spec_match.rationale: Touches JPEG, HDR from the chromestatus summary but skips royalty-free, PIK, FUIF, ISO — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Copy"; "↑"; "↓" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5188299478007808 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.chain-btn:hover:not(:disabled)).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on picture-element-builder
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for JPEG XL decoding support (image/jxl) in blink introduces royalty-free, PIK, FUIF, ISO but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://www.iso.org/standard/77977.html

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on picture-element-builder
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring picture-element-builder back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.chain-btn:hover:not(:disabled)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: picture-element-builder covers the happy path of JPEG XL decoding support (image/jxl) in blink and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
